title: [rootless] `podman pause` idles forever
body:
/kind bug

**Description**
On my system, `podman pause` idles forever under `PID1`.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
``` sh
root@debian:~# pstree --long --compact-not podman
bash

systemd─┬─(sd-pam)
        └─dbus-daemon

# ------------  start container ------------

podman@debian:~$ podman="/home/podman/podman/usr/local/bin/"
podman@debian:~$ "$podman/podman \
--conmon $podman/conmon \
--network-cmd-path $podman/slirp4netns \
--runtime $podman/runc \
--storage-driver overlay"

# ------------  while container is running ------------

root@debian:~# pstree --long --compact-not podman
conmon─┬─bash
       └─{conmon}

podman pause

bash───podman─┬─podman─┬─podman
              │        ├─slirp4netns
              │        ├─{podman}
              │        ├─{podman}
              │        ├─{podman}
              │        ├─{podman}
              │        ├─{podman}
              │        ├─{podman}
              │        ├─{podman}
              │        ├─{podman}
              │        ├─{podman}
              │        ├─{podman}
              │        ├─{podman}
              │        ├─{podman}
              │        └─{podman}
              ├─{podman}
              ├─{podman}
              ├─{podman}
              ├─{podman}
              ├─{podman}
              ├─{podman}
              ├─{podman}
              ├─{podman}
              ├─{podman}
              ├─{podman}
              ├─{podman}
              └─{podman}

systemd─┬─(sd-pam)
        └─dbus-daemon

# ------------  after the container was exited ------------

root@debian:~# pstree --long --compact-not podman
podman pause

bash

systemd─┬─(sd-pam)
        └─dbus-daemon
```

**Describe the results you received:**
`podman pause` never dies.

**Describe the results you expected:**
No `podman` process after last container was exited.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Binaries are only available under user `podman`, there's nothing in root. There is no _podman_ socket and no service installed in _systemd_ (neither root, nor user). `podman` is a fresh user, _lingering_ is activated and the system is under _cgroupsv2_.

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
podman version 3.2.0-dev
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
cpodman2 info --debug
host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.21.0
  cgroupControllers: []
  cgroupManager: systemd
  cgroupVersion: v2
  conmon:
    package: Unknown

    version: 'conmon version 2.0.30-dev, commit: 24c73c2abb3f220ebd97acf498b57c560ca26c04'
  cpus: 8
  distribution:
    distribution: debian
    version: "11"
  eventLogger: journald
  hostname: debian
  idMappings:
    gidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1002
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 231072
      size: 65536
    uidmap:
    - container_id: 0
      host_id: 1002
      size: 1
    - container_id: 1
      host_id: 231072
      size: 65536
  kernel: 5.12.6-051206-generic
  linkmode: dynamic
  memFree: 7789170688
  memTotal: 8339480576
  ociRuntime:
    name: /home/podman/podman/usr/local/bin//crun
    package: Unknown
    path: /home/podman/podman/usr/local/bin//crun
    version: |-
      crun version UNKNOWN
      commit: 8d6a8b5ab80461cfed19f020a36584af13c32038
      spec: 1.0.0
      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux
  remoteSocket:
    path: /run/user/1002/podman/podman.sock
  security:
    apparmorEnabled: false
    capabilities: CAP_AUDIT_WRITE,CAP_CHOWN,CAP_DAC_OVERRIDE,CAP_FOWNER,CAP_FSETID,CAP_KILL,CAP_MKNOD,CAP_NET_BIND_SERVICE,CAP_NET_RAW,CAP_SETFCAP,CAP_SETGID,CAP_SETPCAP,CAP_SETUID,CAP_SYS_CHROOT
    rootless: true
    seccompEnabled: true
    seccompProfilePath: /usr/share/containers/seccomp.json
    selinuxEnabled: false
  serviceIsRemote: false
  slirp4netns:
    executable: ""
    package: ""
    version: ""
  swapFree: 1022357504
  swapTotal: 1022357504
  uptime: 1h 39m 22.64s (Approximately 0.04 days)
registries:
  search:
  - docker.io
  - quay.io
store:
  configFile: /home/podman/.config/containers/storage.conf
  containerStore:
    number: 0
    paused: 0
    running: 0
    stopped: 0
  graphDriverName: overlay
  graphOptions: {}
  graphRoot: /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage
  graphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  imageStore:
    number: 1
  runRoot: /tmp/podman-run-1002/containers
  volumePath: /home/podman/.local/share/containers/storage/volumes
version:
  APIVersion: 3.2.0-dev
  Built: 1622859762
  BuiltTime: Sat Jun  5 02:22:42 2021
  GitCommit: cdf26a3d5677e0372e8892f85a8fd6065d512bcf
  GoVersion: go1.16.5
  OsArch: linux/amd64
  Version: 3.2.0-dev
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
own build
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Debian Bullseye RC1 on EXSi on x86_64

comments.body: I'd still like to revisit this and write a race-safe way of closing the pause process, but it's complex to get right and low-priority since leaking a single process, which consumes virtually no resources, is not really a large issue.
Okay, after reading into the other issues and PRs as well as our talk on IRC, the problem is clear to me now. Thanks.
I am going to close this issue, since we have other reports and are not going to work on it now.
